title: Advanced Sign Language Video Generation with Compressed and Quantized Multi-Condition Tokenization

repos.name: umnooob__signvip
repos.evidence: README header matches paper title exactly and lists all 9 paper authors (Cong Wang, Zexuan Deng, Zhiwei Jiang, Yafeng Yin, Fei Shen, Zifeng Cheng, Shiping Ge, Shiwei Gan, Qing Gu); cites arXiv:2506.15980 and 'NeurIPS 2025 (Spotlight)'. Paper itself links to https://github.com/umnooob/signvip/. Repo contains all three SignViP pipeline stages: train_stage1_multi_cond.py (Sign Video Diffusion Model), train_compress_vq_multicond.py (FSQ Autoencoder), train_multihead_t2vqpgpt.py (Multi-Condition Token Translator), plus models/condition_encoder.py, models/multihead_t2vqpgpt.py, models/vq/, pipelines/pipeline_multicond.py — directly implementing the paper's named components.

rationale: The single cloned repo umnooob/signvip is unambiguously the paper's own implementation: the README reproduces the exact paper title, all 9 author names, the NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight designation, and arXiv:2506.15980, and the paper's own text states 'The code is available at https://github.com/umnooob/signvip/'. The repo implements all three named SignViP components. The audit correctly identified this as the author code and wrote findings against legitimate reproducibility gaps (missing evaluation metric drivers, absent back-translation SLT model, no baseline code, missing How2Sign checkpoints, hardcoded /deepo_data paths). No wrong-repo misidentification occurred.
audit_impact: None. The audit correctly identified the core repo and wrote substantive findings against legitimate gaps in the released evaluation infrastructure. All 9 findings were verified and kept. The paper-level source-present classification is accurate.
recommended_action: No reclassification needed. Audit findings stand as-is.
